name: Materiality gate on lobbying coverage
description: The statute uses some materiality test to scope which individuals/entities are subject to lobbying disclosure. Captures both inclusion-framed criteria ("a person IS a lobbyist if X") and exemption-framed criteria ("a person is NOT subject to disclosure if X"). Rubrics frame this differently: PRI D0 is exemption-framed; Newmark/Opheim/CPI lean inclusion-framed via the compensation/expenditure/time standards in the definition.
framework_references_json: [{"framework": "pri_2010_disclosure", "item_id": "D0", "item_text": "Does the law include a materiality test (ability to exempt) for coverage in disclosure regulations?"}, {"framework": "newmark_2017", "item_id": "def_materiality_umbrella", "item_text": "Newmark 2017's compensation/expenditure/time standards each imply the existence of a materiality test (umbrella)."}, {"framework": "newmark_2005", "item_id": "def_materiality_umbrella", "item_text": "Newmark 2005's compensation/expenditure/time standards each imply a materiality test."}, {"framework": "opheim_1991", "item_id": "def_materiality_umbrella", "item_text": "Opheim 1991's seven definition criteria collectively constitute the materiality umbrella."}, {"framework": "hired_guns_2007", "item_id": "Q2_umbrella", "item_text": "CPI Q2's qualification-threshold tiers presume some materiality test."}]

name: Expenditure-on-lobbying threshold (in statute)
description: The statute exempts persons whose lobbying expenditures fall below a dollar threshold from filing (exemption-framed: "a person whose lobbying expenses < $X is exempt from filing" — PRI D1, FOCAL 1.2). Inclusion-framed counterpart: see DEF_EXPENDITURE_STANDARD ("spends > $X on lobbying IS a lobbyist"). A statute can populate one row, the other, or both — they are independent statutory mechanisms when a state uses an inclusion trigger on one axis with an exemption carve-out on another (e.g., time-trigger + expenditure-exemption).
framework_references_json: [{"framework": "pri_2010_disclosure", "item_id": "D1_present", "item_text": "Financial threshold exists: if expenses are less than some dollar threshold the entity is exempted from filing disclosure."}, {"framework": "focal_2024", "item_id": "1.2", "item_text": "There is no (or low) financial or time threshold to qualify/exempt lobbyists from registration"}, {"framework": "hired_guns_2007", "item_id": "Q2_expenditure", "item_text": "Expenditure portion of CPI Q2 qualification threshold"}]

name: Expenditure-on-lobbying threshold dollar value (USD)
description: Dollar value of the lobbying-expenditure threshold (whether framed as inclusion or exemption).
framework_references_json: [{"framework": "pri_2010_disclosure", "item_id": "D1_value", "item_text": "Dollar threshold value (USD) for financial-threshold exemption."}, {"framework": "hired_guns_2007", "item_id": "Q2_value", "item_text": "Dollar value of qualification threshold (CPI Q2)"}]

name: Time-spent-on-lobbying threshold (in statute)
description: The statute exempts persons whose lobbying time falls below a fraction-of-compensated-time threshold from filing (exemption-framed: "if you spend < X% of compensated time lobbying you are exempt" — PRI D2, FOCAL 1.2). Inclusion-framed counterpart: see DEF_TIME_STANDARD ("> X% of compensated time = you ARE a lobbyist"). A statute can populate one row, the other, or both — they are independent statutory mechanisms when a state uses an inclusion trigger on one axis with an exemption carve-out on another.
framework_references_json: [{"framework": "pri_2010_disclosure", "item_id": "D2_present", "item_text": "Time threshold exists: if amount of time devoted to lobbying is less than a threshold percentage of an individual's compensated time the individual or entity is exempted from filing disclosure."}, {"framework": "focal_2024", "item_id": "1.2", "item_text": "There is no (or low) financial or time threshold to qualify/exempt lobbyists from registration"}]

name: Time-spent-on-lobbying threshold percentage value
description: Percentage value of the lobbying-time threshold (whether framed as inclusion or exemption).
framework_references_json: [{"framework": "pri_2010_disclosure", "item_id": "D2_value", "item_text": "Percentage threshold value for time-threshold exemption."}]

name: Compensation-received threshold in lobbyist definition
description: Statutory definition includes a compensation-received threshold (income-side: paid > $X per period = lobbyist), distinct from PRI D1 expenditure-side threshold.

name: Days lobbying allowed before registration required
description: Maximum number of days a lobbyist may lobby before registration becomes mandatory (delay-to-register, not a fraction-of-time exemption).
framework_references_json: [{"framework": "hired_guns_2007", "item_id": "Q4", "item_text": "How many days can lobbying take place before registration is required?"}]

name: Bill / subject matter required on registration form
description: Registration form requires lobbyist to identify subject matter (general) or specific bill numbers being lobbied.
framework_references_json: [{"framework": "hired_guns_2007", "item_id": "Q5", "item_text": "Is subject matter or bill number to be addressed by a lobbyist required on registration forms?"}]

description: How often a lobbyist must renew or re-file their registration (once / every 2 years / annually or more).
framework_references_json: [{"framework": "hired_guns_2007", "item_id": "Q6", "item_text": "How often is registration by a lobbyist required?"}]

name: Expenditure-on-lobbying standard in lobbyist definition (inclusion-framed)
description: Statutory definition includes a lobbying-expenditure threshold as an inclusion-side criterion: 'a person who spends > $X on lobbying IS a lobbyist' (Newmark/Opheim 'expenditure standard'). Exemption-framed counterpart: see THRESHOLD_LOBBYING_EXPENDITURE_PRESENT ('expenses < $X exempt from filing' — PRI D1). A statute can populate one row, the other, or both — they are independent statutory mechanisms when a state combines an inclusion trigger on one axis with an exemption carve-out on another.
framework_references_json: [{"framework": "newmark_2017", "item_id": "def_expenditure_standard", "item_text": "Expenditure standard"}, {"framework": "newmark_2005", "item_id": "def_expenditure_standard", "item_text": "Expenditure standard"}, {"framework": "opheim_1991", "item_id": "def_expenditure_standard", "item_text": "Expenditure standard"}]

name: Time-spent-on-lobbying standard in lobbyist definition (inclusion-framed)
description: Statutory definition includes a fraction-of-compensated-time threshold as an inclusion-side criterion: 'a person who spends > X% of compensated time on lobbying IS a lobbyist' (Newmark/Opheim 'time standard'). Exemption-framed counterpart: see THRESHOLD_LOBBYING_TIME_PRESENT ('< X% time exempt from filing' — PRI D2). A statute can populate one row, the other, or both — they are independent statutory mechanisms when a state combines an inclusion trigger on one axis with an exemption carve-out on another.
framework_references_json: [{"framework": "newmark_2017", "item_id": "def_time_standard", "item_text": "Time standard"}, {"framework": "newmark_2005", "item_id": "def_time_standard", "item_text": "Time standard"}, {"framework": "opheim_1991", "item_id": "def_time_standard", "item_text": "Time standard"}]
